trans-unit.source: Manage user accounts, roles, and pending grants.
Edit site content and published pages.
Manage track-scoped challenge prompts for idea submission.
Something went wrong. Please try again.
No escalations match the current filters.
Grant a Role to an AD Group
A full trail of administrative and workflow actions.
All submitted ideas and their current status.
Report generation failed. Please try again.
Grant a Role by Account Name
There are no pending role grants.
Role will apply on their first login.
Unable to load your identity. Please refresh and try again.
There are no ideas awaiting committee review.
You haven't submitted any committee decisions yet.
There are no ideas awaiting your evaluation.
You haven't submitted any evaluations yet.
Add at least one attachment before submitting.
Add at least one attachment before submitting to committee.
We found a saved draft. Would you like to restore it?
Add at least 2 members in addition to you as the team leader.
Drag files here or click to choose
PDF, images, Office files, or video — up to 10MB each, 5 files max
I confirm I am the author of this idea and responsible for it.
I agree to the program's terms and conditions.
You can attach up to 5 files.
One or more files have a type that isn't allowed.
One or more files are larger than 10MB.
You haven't created any ideas yet.
Your idea could change how competition works in Saudi Arabia — present it before experts and specialists, and compete for valuable prizes.
The current submission window is closed.
Innovation to Impact brings together employees, entrepreneurs, and government entities to turn innovative ideas into practical solutions with real impact on the sector.
Our mission: accelerate the journey from idea to execution through one unified, transparent platform.
An open competition on a digital platform, spanning submission, evaluation, pitching, and final decision stages.
Open to Authority employees, entrepreneurs, government entities, and innovation specialists.
Every submission is measured against five weighted criteria — balancing boldness of the idea with how well it can actually ship and shift the market.
The previous edition saw wide participation, with several ideas adopted and actually implemented.
The video will be added soon
Don't overthink it — just tell us what you're thinking.
Foster a culture of institutional innovation
Accelerate turning ideas into actionable initiatives
Strengthen collaboration across teams and entities
Build a reusable knowledge base of solutions
Measure the real-world impact of implemented ideas
Register individually or as a team of up to 5 members
Every idea must fall under one of the approved tracks
Each team member signs the IP terms independently
Register individually or as a team, and choose your track.
Final deadline to receive participation requests.
Applications are screened and teams selected.
Solutions are presented to the judging panel.
How novel and differentiated the idea is versus what already exists.
Expected value to competition, consumers, and the wider economy.
Clarity of the plan, realism of the resources and the timeline.
Ability to expand geographically or across sectors without rebuilding.
Sharpness of the summary, strength of the evidence, and pitch quality.
King Abdulaziz City for Science and Technology
Employees of the Authority, entrepreneurs, government entities, and innovation specialists are all welcome to submit.
Initial check: a few working days. Full evaluation: two to three weeks.
Yes. Ideas are handled according to the confidentiality level and reviewed only by authorized evaluators and committee members.
Can I submit more than one idea?
Yes, there is no limit on the number of ideas you may submit.
What happens if my idea needs changes?
You'll get feedback on what needs work and can update your idea and resubmit.
Do I retain ownership of my idea?
Ownership terms are described in the IP terms you acknowledge when submitting.
Will I be notified of progress?
Yes — you'll get a notification every time something happens with your idea.
Ideas are scored on strategic alignment, innovation, feasibility, impact, and effort. See the Evaluation Criteria page.
The unified innovation platform of the General Authority for Competition — turning ideas into measurable impact.
General Authority for Competition, Riyadh, Kingdom of Saudi Arabia
© General Authority for Competition · Innovation to Impact — All rights reserved
There are no ideas awaiting screening.
Configure automatic invitation reminders and defaults.
Submit your idea now and follow it up in My Ideas.
No ideas yet. Submit your first one!
Get your ideas approved by the committee.
We'll notify you as your idea moves through review.
A supervisor reviews your idea for completeness.
Evaluators score your idea against the published criteria.
You'll be notified of the committee's decision by email and in-app.
Review ideas assigned to your tracks and submit your evaluations.
Tracks appear in the idea submission wizard and can be bound to evaluators.
Set start and end dates for each of the 7 program phases.
Automatically attached to every email using this template.
A suggestion only — you choose who to assign.
Assign evaluators to ideas, monitor workload, and manage reassignment.
This person already has this role
An invitation for this person and role is already pending
Columns: samAccountName, role. The first row may optionally be a header row.
The minimum average evaluation score (0–10) an idea must reach to pass technical evaluation. Applies to future evaluations.
Advance approved ideas through pilot, measurement and scaling.
An idea and an evaluator are required.
Arabic and English names are required.
Innovation to Impact — how the General Authority for Competition turns ideas into real results.
To build a culture of innovation across the General Authority for Competition — where every employee can shape a fairer, more competitive Saudi market aligned with Vision 2030.
To make sure good ideas from anywhere in GAC actually get built — with a fair process and visible results.

Every idea follows the same path — submitted, reviewed by experts, decided by the committee, piloted, then rolled out. At each step, someone is responsible and the criteria are published, so you always know where your idea stands.
The platform is open to every part of the Authority.
From frontline staff to leadership — anyone with an idea that can improve how the Authority delivers on its mandate.
Legal, economic, market monitoring, technology, communications, HR, and every other function across the Authority.
Submit on your own or bring an idea forward as a team — the pipeline supports both individual and collaborative submissions.
We collaborate with entities across government, academia, and the private sector.
Got a question about the program or want to talk through an idea before submitting? Drop us a line.
How submitted ideas are assessed by evaluators and the committee.
How closely the idea supports the Authority's strategic themes and national priorities.
Originality and the degree to which the idea improves on existing approaches.
Technical and operational practicality within available resources and timeframe.
Expected scale and significance of the outcome if implemented.
Cost, complexity, and effort required relative to the expected benefit.
Each evaluator scores 1 to 5 per criterion. Strong ideas advance automatically; borderline ones go to the committee.
The flagship annual competition where top ideas are showcased and awarded. Winning ideas are fast-tracked to piloting.
An intensive multi-day event where cross-functional teams prototype solutions to competition-related challenges.
Hands-on sessions to strengthen ideas and build innovation skills.
Competitions, hackathons, and workshops throughout the program cycle.
The flagship annual competition where top ideas are showcased and awarded. Winning ideas are fast-tracked to piloting.
An intensive multi-day event where cross-functional teams prototype solutions to competition-related challenges.
Hands-on sessions to strengthen ideas and build innovation skills.
Examples of the kinds of solutions the program seeks across priority areas.
Data platforms to detect anti-competitive behavior
AI for document and case analysis
Answers to common questions about the program.
Employees of the Authority, entrepreneurs, government entities, and innovation specialists are all welcome to submit.
Initial check: a few working days. Full evaluation: two to three weeks.
Yes. Ideas are handled according to the confidentiality level and reviewed only by authorized evaluators and committee members.
Can I submit more than one idea?
Yes, there is no limit on the number of ideas you may submit.
What happens if my idea needs changes?
You'll get feedback on what needs work and can update your idea and resubmit.
Do I retain ownership of my idea?
Ownership terms are described in the IP terms you acknowledge when submitting.
Will I be notified of progress?
Yes — you'll get a notification every time something happens with your idea.
Ideas are scored on strategic alignment, innovation, feasibility, impact, and effort. See the Evaluation Criteria page.
You can reach the innovation team via the Support page for guidance at any stage.
Submission windows are announced on the Roadmap page and reflected in the countdown on the home page.
Terms and conditions governing ownership of ideas submitted to the Competition Program of the General Authority for Competition.

"Idea" means any proposal, suggestion, innovation or solution submitted by a participant through the GAC Innovation Platform, whether written, visual, or accompanied by supporting documents. "Intellectual Property" includes any patent rights, copyrights, trademarks, trade secrets, industrial designs, and any related rights arising from the idea.
By submitting your idea, you agree that all intellectual property rights transfer to the General Authority for Competition.

The participant warrants that the submitted idea is their original work and that it does not infringe upon any third-party intellectual property rights. The participant bears full responsibility for any legal claims arising from allegations of infringement.

GAC is committed to maintaining the confidentiality of submitted ideas according to the level of confidentiality designated by the participant (public, internal, or confidential). Confidential ideas are accessed only by the authorized evaluation team, solely for screening and evaluation purposes.
GAC acknowledges the participant as the original author of the idea and may credit them in implementation documents and related publications, at its discretion.
If your idea is implemented, GAC may recognise you with a reward — financial or otherwise. Submitting doesn't automatically entitle you to one; any rewards follow GAC's incentive policy.

The ownership transfer terms do not apply to ideas submitted by external rights holders under pre-existing partnership or licensing agreements, or ideas submitted by other government entities subject to their own regulations. In such cases, ownership is determined per written agreement.

By submitting your idea through the platform and agreeing to these terms, you acknowledge that you have read, understood, and agreed to all the terms above, and that you waive any right to claim ownership of the idea after submission as specified.

These terms are governed by the laws of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, particularly the Copyright Law, the Patents and Industrial Designs Law, the Trademarks Law, and the Competition Law. The competent courts in Riyadh have jurisdiction over any dispute arising from the application of these terms.
By clicking "I agree" on the idea submission page, you acknowledge that you have read and agreed to all the terms above.
We collaborate with entities across government, academia, and the private sector.
King Abdulaziz City for Science and Technology
Run real experiments and capture what worked.
This Privacy Policy explains how the General Authority for Competition collects, uses, and protects personal information submitted through the Innovation to Impact platform.
We collect only the information necessary to receive, evaluate, and act on submitted ideas, including your name, contact details, and the content of your submission.
Your information is accessible only to authorized personnel involved in evaluation and program administration, and is never sold or shared for marketing purposes.
We retain submission data for as long as necessary to fulfill the purposes described here and to comply with applicable regulations.
You may request access to or correction of your personal information by contacting the innovation team through the Support page.
Key phases and dates for the current program cycle.
Idea intake begins across all audiences.
Completeness and fit checks on incoming ideas.
Here's how an idea moves from your head to something that actually changes how GAC operates.
You submit your idea. It gets a reference number and enters the queue.
Tell us your idea in your own words. Attach anything that helps explain it.
The team checks your submission meets the basics before sending it to specialist evaluators.
Participate in events and campaigns linked to priorities.
Relevant experts read your idea and score it independently across five dimensions.
Watch where your idea is in the process. The team may reach out if they need more from you.
The committee reviews the strongest ideas and makes the final call on each one.
You may be asked for additional information or a short pitch.
Once approved, the idea gets an owner, a budget, and a delivery date.
Connect with the assigned owner to implement your idea.
The idea runs as a real experiment — tested in a real context before full rollout.
Participate in the pilot and give feedback on results.
What actually changed? This stage captures the real-world difference the idea made.
Collaborate with the adoption team for a wider rollout.
What worked gets rolled out properly — so the improvement reaches everyone it should.
See your idea's impact on strategic objectives.
Thank you — your message has been recorded. The team will reach out shortly.
Get in touch with the innovation team.
General Authority for Competition, Riyadh, Kingdom of Saudi Arabia
The program is open to anyone with a good idea.
Founders and emerging companies with market-ready solutions relevant to competition, market monitoring, and consumer protection.
Public-sector teams seeking to co-develop regulatory and analytical tools, and to pilot cross-agency initiatives.
Researchers, data scientists, and domain experts who can strengthen ideas with evidence, methods, and technical depth.
By using the Innovation to Impact platform you agree to these Terms & Conditions.
You are responsible for the accuracy of the information you submit and for ensuring you have the right to share it.
Submitted ideas are subject to the intellectual-property terms acknowledged at submission time.
The Authority may accept, request revision of, reject, or escalate any submission at its discretion based on the published evaluation criteria.
The platform is provided on an "as is" basis; the Authority may update these terms and program details from time to time.
Browse the activities running throughout the competition and see how many ideas each has attracted.
Grouping ideas by track lets specialized evaluators focus their expertise, and lets successful pilots scale within a coherent theme.
Identify a real challenge within the track's scope
Measure the expected impact on competition
This track focuses submissions on a specific strategic priority, helping evaluators assess alignment and impact within a shared scope.
Explore the themes shaping this year's competition and find where your idea fits best.
Search ideas and tracks across the program.

Triage and resolve inbound support messages.

Manage the weighted criteria committees use to score ideas.
Manage the role catalog used across the platform.
Edit the terms and conditions content page.
Active criteria weights should sum to 1.0 (currently ).
